commands_EXAMPLE.comment: Commands create aliases/functions that invoke scripts. Key is the command you type in terminal, Value is the path to the script (relative to repo root). Example: 'greet' would let you type 'greet' in terminal and it runs scripts/hello.sh. Args are passed through.

exports_EXAMPLE.comment: Exports add environment variables to your shell. Available variables: {SCRIPT_DIR} is replaced with the repo path. Example sets MY_VAR to a custom path.

shell_configs_EXAMPLE.comment: Shell configs add raw lines to your .zshrc/.bashrc. Examples: EDITOR sets your default terminal editor (nvim, vim, code, etc). LANG sets locale/language encoding.
